heading: Standardized reporting format
label: 1191d Standardized reporting format
notes: Change of NameCommittee on Education and Labor of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Education and the Workforce of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Eighteenth Congress, .Jan. 9, 2023

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish (and periodically update) a standardized reporting format for the voluntary reporting, by group health plans to State All Payer Claims Databases, of medical claims, pharmacy claims, dental claims, and eligibility and provider files that are collected from private and public payers, and shall provide guidance to States on the process by which States may collect such data from such plans in the standardized reporting format.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Advisory Committee
children.label: 1 Advisory Committee
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall convene an Advisory Committee (referred to in this section as the “Committee”), consisting of 15 members to advise the Secretary regarding the format and guidance described in paragraph (1).December 27, 20201So in original. Probably should be “subsection (a).”1

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: A Appointment
children.text: In accordance with subparagraph (B), not later than 90 days after , the Secretary, in coordination with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall appoint under subparagraph (B)(iii), and the Comptroller General of the United States shall appoint under subparagraph (B)(iv), members who have distinguished themselves in the fields of health services research, health economics, health informatics, data privacy and security, or the governance of State All Payer Claims Databases, or who represent organizations likely to submit data to or use the database, including patients, employers, or employee organizations that sponsor group health plans, health care providers, health insurance issuers, or third-party administrators of group health plans. Such members shall serve 3-year terms on a staggered basis. Vacancies on the Committee shall be filled by appointment consistent with this paragraph not later than 3 months after the vacancy arises.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: the Assistant Secretary of Employee Benefits and Security Administration of the Department of Labor, or a designee of such Assistant Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation of the Department of Health and Human Services, or a designee of such Assistant Secretary;

children.label: I
children.text: 1 member to serve as the chair of the Committee;

children.label: II
children.text: 1 representative of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services;

children.label: III
children.text: 1 representative of the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality;

children.label: IV
children.text: 1 representative of the Office for Civil Rights of the Department of Health and Human Services with expertise in data privacy and security;

children.label: V
children.text: 1 representative of the National Center for Health Statistics;

children.label: VI
children.text: 1 representative of the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology; and

children.label: VII
children.text: 1 representative of a State All-Payer Claims Database;2So in original. Definition in subsec. (c) does not contain hyphen in “All Payer”.2

children.label: I
children.text: 1 representative of an employer that sponsors a group health plan;

children.label: II
children.text: 1 representative of an employee organization that sponsors a group health plan;

children.label: III
children.text: 1 academic researcher with expertise in health economics or health services research;

children.label: IV
children.text: 1 consumer advocate; and

children.label: V
children.text: 2 additional members.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Committee shall report to the Secretary, the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives. Such report shall include recommendations on the establishment of the format and guidance described in subsection (a).December 27, 2020

children.heading: State All Payer Claims Database
children.label: c State All Payer Claims Database
children.text: In this section, the term “State All Payer Claims Database” means, with respect to a State, a database that may include medical claims, pharmacy claims, dental claims, and eligibility and provider files, which are collected from private and public payers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for fiscal year 2021, to remain available until expended or, if sooner, until the date described in subsection (e).

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: e Sunset
children.text: Beginning on the date on which the report is submitted under subsection (b)(3), subsection (b) shall have no force or effect.
